log_entries.thought_preview: The malware is a PE32 executable, likely a Windows executable with a GUI, and has been stripped to an external PDB. It has a large size of 793675 bytes. The tags indicate that it has runtime modules,
The malware has a wide range of capabilities, including data manipulation, registry access, file system interaction, process management, and more. It uses various techniques such as XOR encoding, PEB

The malware drops several files, including Tmp_.exe, Mkopg.exe, Oiht400.dll, and _exe1.#, in various locations on the system. These files are likely used for different purposes in the malware's behavi
